description:
Today: reduce_terminator's Term::Call branch already handles the case
where the callee folds but the continuation doesn't — it rewrites to
\`TailCall(cont.fn_id, [literal_var, ...captured])\`. That's already
correct.

The failure happens DEEPER: when reduce_terminator's Term::Call calls
\`try_reduce_call\`, that may succeed at the top callee but recurse
into the callee's body, where a NESTED Call/CallClosure's continuation
fails to fold (e.g. apply1's TailCallClosure dispatches to fn14 → 42,
but THEN — wait, this is the top-level case. Re-investigation needed
in B.2 game-walk.)

This ticket: investigate WHY closure_typed_captures' apply1 call
fails to fold today. The Stalled outcome from B.2 captures the
failure point. Document in a comment. Decide if it's:
(a) Already correct — the chain genuinely can't fold.
(b) A reducer coverage bug — partial fold needed at a deeper layer.

If (b): implement the deeper partial fold. Acceptance: apply1's
callsite becomes Consumed, surviving bodies shrink.

## Acceptance

- One of:
  (a) Doc-only: explanation of why partial fold can't help.
  (b) closure_typed_captures: apply1 callsite is Consumed; fewer
      surviving spec keys; runtime output unchanged.
- All 842 tests green.

## Removes

Nothing. Improves reducer coverage.

comments.text: Game-walk on closure_typed_captures: main's Term::Call(apply1, [c, twelve], k2) appears to have all the ingredients to fold (closure_lit captures literal, args literal, fn14 body folds to 42), but the survivor list shows apply1 still called. Investigation hypothesis: apply1's entry block has a structure (perhaps a dispatcher with match_error fail-block) that walk_block can't navigate. Confirm by reading the actual lowered IR; document why; decide if partial fold helps or if the issue is somewhere else.

title: fz-CO.C.1 — Partial fold for Term::Call when cont doesn't fold
